rationale: The citing paper uses Spiller et al. (2013) as an explicit methodological guide for probing interaction effects with Johnson-Neyman floodlight analysis. It relies on this approach in core hypothesis tests (notably Studies 1 and 3) to determine the self-construal ranges where positive versus negative norm deviations differ significantly. Because the citation shapes how central moderated effects are tested and interpreted, it is more than a passing background reference.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Spiller et al.'s spotlight/floodlight simple-effects framework, especially Johnson-Neyman probing, to analyze and interpret its key interaction results.
